key_points: Agriculture is the practice of cultivating crops and raising animals
India has three cropping seasons: Rabi, Kharif, and Zaid
Rice is the staple food crop of India
Wheat is the second most important cereal crop in India
Millets require less rainfall and grow in dry regions
examples: Rice is grown in areas with high rainfall
Wheat is grown in northern plains like Punjab and Haryana
Millets include jowar, bajra, and ragi
Tea plantations are common in Assam
Cotton grows well in black soil regions
formulas: Cropping Season Types = Rabi + Kharif + Zaid
Agriculture Output depends on Climate + Soil + Water
exam_tips: Remember the three cropping seasons in India
Learn the major crops and their climatic conditions
Understand the difference between Rabi and Kharif crops
Focus on examples of crops and their growing regions
